title: Private Tutoring Company Profit Decline
background: A private tutoring company serves middle school and high school students through online and in-person sessions. Revenue has increased, but profits are down because the company has hired more tutors and expanded marketing.
candidate_prompt: How would you determine whether the company’s growth is profitable and what changes should be made?
good_answer_includes: Analyzes revenue by student type and service format
Examines marketing costs and customer acquisition
great_answer_includes: Calculates tutor utilization and contribution margin per session
Segments customers by acquisition channel and retention
Evaluates pricing differences across subjects or formats
Recommends scheduling, pricing, and marketing efficiency improvements
interviewer_notes: Strong candidates should focus on unit economics by session, tutor utilization, and customer acquisition payback.
